findings.detail: All SQL queries use parameterized statements with ? placeholders. No string concatenation or f-string interpolation in SQL. Examples: conn.execute('SELECT * FROM shadows WHERE id = ?', (shadow_id,)), conn.execute('UPDATE shadows SET status = ? WHERE id = ?', (status, shadow_id)). Verified across all 30+ SQL operations.

save_beta_analyses() writes to isolated beta_analyses table (separate from main analyses table). get_ranking_eligible_shadows() excludes beta, retired, eliminated, and challenger shadows. get_visible_shadows() additionally excludes challengers. Beta outputs NEVER leak into ranking/collusion/challenger pipelines.

ShadowConfig serialization uses json.dumps() on a hardcoded dict of fields — no user-controlled keys. Deserialization uses json.loads() with try/except JSONDecodeError, falling back to empty config defaults. No eval/exec.

WAL mode, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000ms, foreign_keys=ON. Data safety: WAL prevents corruption on crash, busy_timeout prevents immediate failures under contention. No in-memory DB — persistent file.

init_schema() checks schema_version from metadata table. Only applies migrations with version > db_version. Upward-only. Warns (not crashes) if db_version > CODE_VERSION (database opened with newer code). ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN uses try/except for idempotency.

Shadow data contains: shadow IDs, display names, methodology prompts, virtual capital, trade records, performance metrics. No real PII, no account numbers, no real capital. Virtual trading ecosystem only.
datetime.now(timezone.utc) used consistently for: retire_shadow, eliminate_shadow, update_methodology_prompt, save_beta_analyses. No bare datetime.now() calls.
